remember: Students will be able to recall the accounts of various travellers who visited India during different periods of history.
Students will be able to identify the key features of the accounts of travellers such as Ibn Battuta, Niccolao Manucci, and François Bernier.
Students will be able to list the different regions of India visited by travellers and describe their accounts of these regions.
understand: Students will be able to understand the significance of the accounts of travellers in understanding Indian history and culture.
Students will be able to explain how the accounts of travellers provide valuable information about the social, economic, and political conditions of India during different periods.
Students will be able to analyze the biases and limitations of the accounts of travellers and their impact on our understanding of Indian history.
apply: Students will be able to apply the knowledge of the accounts of travellers to understand the historical events and processes that shaped India.
Students will be able to use the accounts of travellers to compare and contrast the different regions of India and their cultural practices.
Students will be able to evaluate the credibility and reliability of the accounts of travellers and use them as primary sources for historical research.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the cultural, social, and economic changes that took place in India during the period of interaction with European travellers.
Students will be able to compare the accounts of different travellers to identify the common themes and patterns that emerged during their visits to India.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of the accounts of travellers on our understanding of Indian history and culture.

ncert_objectives: To understand the significance of the accounts of travellers in understanding Indian history and culture.
To analyze the cultural, social, and economic changes that took place in India during the period of interaction with European travellers.
To evaluate the credibility and reliability of the accounts of travellers and use them as primary sources for historical research.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the key features of the accounts of travellers such as Ibn Battuta, Niccolao Manucci, and François Bernier.
To explain how the accounts of travellers provide valuable information about the social, economic, and political conditions of India during different periods.
To analyze the biases and limitations of the accounts of travellers and their impact on our understanding of Indian history.
